label: claim 1
claim 2
claim 3
text: **Claim 3.** The system of claim 2, wherein the per-measurer trust weight is computed by an event-log-reading aggregator configured to (i) read, from the append-only event log of claim 1, a time-ordered sequence of refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to each measurer, (ii) compute a decay-weighted aggregate of said signals over a configurable time window, and (iii) emit the aggregated value as the trust-weight argument optionally consumed by the measurer-correlation analyser of claim 1, such that the trust-weight loop operates through the event log rather than through mutation of prior coupling entries.

label: claim 4
text: **Claim 4.** The system of claim 1, wherein the convergence analyser is further configured, for any (target, property) group in which all records in said group carry the source tag indicating computation, to suppress classification of said group as *converged* and to classify said group instead as *single*, such that a group lacking any physical-interaction record is architecturally incapable of achieving a *converged* classification.

label: claim 5
text:
**Claim 5.** The system of claim 1, wherein the measurer-correlation analyser is further configured, for any given measurer, to:

- partition said measurer's groups by property identifier;
- identify at least one property subset in which a specified fraction of said measurer's groups on that property subset are classified as *divergent* or *tension*, said fraction exceeding a first threshold; and
- identify at least one property subset in which a specified fraction of said measurer's groups on that property subset are classified as *converged*, said fraction falling below a second threshold distinct from the first,

and to emit, when both such subsets exist for the same measurer, a universal-co-divergence signal identifying said measurer together with the divergent and non-divergent property subsets, said signal being routed through the feedback-signal interface to the actor.

label: claim 6
text:
**Claim 6.** The system of claim 1, wherein the convergence analyser and the measurer-correlation analyser are further configured to process groups containing both at least one record produced by a physical measurement apparatus and at least one record produced by a computational measurer, by:

- computing the relative-spread measure of claim 1 using all records in said group regardless of source tag;
- classifying said group under claim 1's four-valued classification using said relative-spread measure; and
- when said classification is *converged*, gating subsequent admission of additional computational records into said group on the continued presence of at least one physical-interaction record in said group,

such that a computational-record majority in a group classified as *converged* does not suppress admission of further physical-interaction records, and such that removal of the last physical-interaction record from a *converged* group causes re-classification of said group under the group-consists-entirely-of-computational-records rule of claim 4.

label: claim 7
text:
**Claim 7.** The system of claim 1, wherein the actor is one or more of:

- a human operator consuming said feedback signal through a human-machine interface and responding by modifying at least one of a sampling schedule, a sensor-selection parameter, or a data-gating parameter of the physical measurement apparatus;
- a human operator consuming said feedback signal and responding by annotating the storage with an additional directed entry; or
- an automated control subsystem consuming said feedback signal without human intermediation.

label: claim 8
text: **Claim 8.** The system of claim 7, wherein the actor is an automated control subsystem configured, in response to the feedback signal, to modify operation of the physical measurement apparatus of claim 1 without requiring human input, wherein modifying operation comprises at least one of: altering a sampling schedule of the physical measurement apparatus; altering a sensor-selection or sensor-gain parameter of the physical measurement apparatus; or altering a data-gating parameter controlling admission of further physical-interaction records from the physical measurement apparatus into the storage.

label: claim 8a
claim 9
text:
**Claim 9.** A computer-implemented scientific knowledge analysis system comprising:

- a vocabulary store configured to hold an operational-mechanism vocabulary comprising language-independent numerical identifiers, wherein each identifier in the vocabulary carries a Boolean admissibility flag having a first value indicating that the identifier names a physical or computational mechanism embodied by a measurement instrument, and a second value indicating that the identifier names a theoretical commitment about reality or an assumption about a target of measurement, and wherein the vocabulary store is configured to reject, at insertion time, any identifier whose admissibility flag takes the second value, and wherein any consumer of the vocabulary store is constrained to read only identifiers whose admissibility flag takes the first value;
- a storage configured to hold a plurality of inference steps, each said inference step comprising (i) a claim descriptor, (ii) a convergence-status annotation selected from at least *converged*, *tension*, and *divergent*, and (iii) a list of admissible operational-mechanism identifiers drawn from the vocabulary store; and
- an inference-step consumer configured to process the plurality of inference steps under a reading discipline that does not admit any identifier whose admissibility flag takes the second value,

**characterised in that** the vocabulary store enforces admissibility by schema-level validation such that inadmissible identifiers are architecturally incapable of entering the reading path of the inference-step consumer, and such that any inference drawn by the inference-step consumer from shared identifiers across distinct inference steps reflects shared instrument mechanism and does not reflect shared theoretical framing.

label: claim 10
claim 11
text: **Claim 11.** The system of claim 10, wherein the marker-propagation analyser is further configured to bound the graph traversal to a fixed hop-count limit and to terminate propagation when marker intensity falls below a specified decay threshold, such that the worst-case time complexity of collision detection is sub-quadratic in the number of operational-mechanism identifiers in the vocabulary store.

label: claim 12
text: **Claim 12.** The system of claim 10, wherein the storage of claim 9 further holds a plurality of measurer entities drawn from the same identifier space as the target entities of the inference steps, such that a measurer entity is representable as the target of an inference step produced by another measurer, and wherein the marker-propagation analyser is configured to propagate markers through inference steps in which a measurer appears as target without schema change to the storage.

label: claim 13
claim 14
claim 15
claim 16
claim 16a
claim 17
text: **Claim 17.** A non-transitory computer-readable medium carrying instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform the method of any one of claims 13, 14, 15, 16, or 16a.

label: claim 20
text:
**20.** The system of claim 1, wherein:

- the source tag further takes a third predetermined value distinct from the first two said values, the third said value indicating that the record was produced by an automated actor performing an action on the target entity;
- each said record whose source tag carries the third said value further comprises one or more structured pointers, each said structured pointer identifying a coupling entry in the storage whose content was read by the automated actor prior to producing the action and upon which the action was conditioned; and
- the storage records said action records in the same six-field schema as records whose source tag carries the first or second said values.

label: claim 21
claim 22
text:
**22.** The system of claim 21, further comprising a loop-gain analyzer configured to:

- identify, from a directed graph induced by the records in the storage and by the structured pointers of claim 20, one or more cyclic subgraphs, each said cyclic subgraph comprising at least one record whose source tag carries the third predetermined value and at least one record whose source tag carries one of the first two predetermined values;
- compute, for each said cyclic subgraph, a spectral-radius metric obtained from a linearization of the subgraph's dynamics in the neighbourhood of a current state; and
- emit, for each said cyclic subgraph whose spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds a predetermined threshold, a typed cycle annotation stored in the append-only event log of claim 1.

label: claim 23
claim 24
claim 25
text:
**25.** The system of claim 1, further comprising an uncertainty-propagation layer configured to:

- receive, for a target entity's history in the storage, an empirical-distribution characterization of the target entity's measurement values;
- select, based on said empirical-distribution characterization, one of a plurality of uncertainty-propagation methods, said plurality comprising at least a first method assuming approximately Gaussian distributions of measurement error and a second method robust to distributions exhibiting heavy-tailed or multi-modal characteristics; and
- record the selection in the append-only event log as a typed annotation.

label: claim 26
claim 27
text:
**27.** The system of claim 1, wherein:

- the storage further accommodates, for each target entity, a typed-absence annotation taking one of at least four predetermined values when no measurement record exists for a specific property of the target entity, the four said values distinguishing absences by their origin within a predetermined taxonomy;
- the storage further accommodates, for each target entity, an actionability tier annotation taking one of a predetermined plurality of values indicating the degree to which the target entity is available for action records to be produced referencing it; and
- the analyzers of claims 1, 22, 24, and 25 are configured to respect said typed-absence and actionability-tier annotations in their emissions.

label: claim 28
claim 29
claim 30
claim 31
text: **31.** The system of claim 29 or claim 30, wherein the ranked list emitted by the counterfactual generator or the experimental-design generator is further filtered by the actionability-tier annotation of claim 27, such that candidate action records or candidate counterfactual histories referencing target entities whose actionability-tier annotation indicates non-actionable status are removed from the ranked list or routed to a human-review queue in lieu of emission to an automated-action pathway.

label: claim 32
text:
**32.** The system comprising claim 29 and claim 30, further comprising a routing component configured to:

- receive, from the convergence analyzer of claim 1 or the loop-gain analyzer of claim 22, a detected crack or a detected cyclic subgraph;
- determine, from a dynamical-signature annotation of claim 23 if present or from the absence of such an annotation, whether to dispatch the detected crack or cyclic subgraph to the counterfactual generator of claim 29, to the experimental-design generator of claim 30, or to both in parallel; and
- record, in the append-only event log of claim 1, a typed dispatch event specifying the detected crack or cyclic subgraph, the dispatch decision, and the generator or generators selected.

label: claim 33
text:
**33.** The method of claim 13, wherein:

- the source tag further takes a third predetermined value distinct from the first two said values, the third said value indicating that the record was produced by an automated actor performing an action on the target entity; and
- producing each action record comprises recording, in the same six-field schema as records whose source tag carries the first or second said values, one or more structured pointers identifying coupling entries whose content was read by the automated actor prior to producing the action and upon which the action was conditioned.

label: claim 34
text:
**34.** The method of claim 33, further comprising:

- identifying, from a directed graph induced by the records produced in accordance with claim 13 and the structured pointers of claim 33, one or more cyclic subgraphs, each said cyclic subgraph comprising at least one record whose source tag carries the third predetermined value and at least one record whose source tag carries one of the first two predetermined values;
- computing, for each said cyclic subgraph, a spectral-radius metric obtained from a linearization of the subgraph's dynamics in the neighbourhood of a current state; and
- emitting, for each said cyclic subgraph whose spectral-radius metric meets or exceeds a predetermined threshold, a typed cycle annotation.

label: claim 35
text: **35.** A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions which, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform the method of any of claims 13, 33, and 34.

label: Rauf 2025 (Gem entity-splitting framework)
text: Rauf, W.; Bogatu, A.; Paton, N. W.; Freitas, A. "Gem: Large-scale schema-agnostic entity resolution." Proceedings of the 28th International Conference on Extending Database Technology (EDBT 2025). — Anticipates GMM-based distributional clustering for entity-type classification. Claim 1's population-decomposition analyser is distinguished by: (i) tension → decompose → split → storage as a strict causal chain; (ii) minimum-component-size and minimum-inter-component-separation guards beyond BIC alone; (iii) identifier-aliasing in the event log preserving parent-entity classifications; (iv) split effected as a feedback action into the measurement store. Specification §4.6.

label: Fahlman 2006 (Scone marker-passing inference)
text: Fahlman, S. E. "Marker-Passing Inference in the Scone Knowledge-Base System." Carnegie Mellon University School of Computer Science Technical Report, 2006. — Prior art for marker-passing. Claim 9 is distinguished by schema-enforced admissibility filtering of the operational-mechanism vocabulary, which Scone does not enforce. Claim 10's collision classifier is distinguished by the opening-collision / crack-collision / bridge-collision taxonomy, which no prior marker-passing system produces. Specification §4.8, §4.9.

label: Norvig 1989 (Norvig marker-collision taxonomy)
text: Norvig, P. "Marker passing as a weak method for text inferencing." Cognitive Science 13(4):569–620, 1989. — Prior art for marker collision. Same distinguishing points as Scone (v6.1.2).

label: US 7,962,495 B2
text: U.S. Patent No. 7,962,495 B2, "Creating data in a data store using a dynamic ontology," Palantir Technologies Inc., granted 14 June 2011. — Anticipates measurer-as-entity under an ontology layer. Claim 12 retains measurer-as-entity specifically bound to the mechanism-1 marker-propagation dependency-graph side. Claim 1 (and claim 13) ontology disclaimer explicitly excludes an object-type or property-type registry (tightened in v6.2). Specification §4.15.

label: US 9,589,014 B2
text: U.S. Patent No. 9,589,014 B2, "Dynamic language model," Palantir Technologies Inc., granted 7 March 2017. — Same family as US 7,962,495. Same mitigation.

label: US 10,180,929 B1
text: U.S. Patent No. 10,180,929 B1, "Ontology-based object-type mapping," Palantir Technologies Inc., granted 15 January 2019. U.S. Patent No. 10,803,106 B1, "System with modular object management," Palantir Technologies Inc., granted 13 October 2020. — FTO-adjacent Palantir modular-ontology patents. Claim 1 and claim 13 tightened disclaimer item (i) carves out ontology-layer subject matter and item (ii) carves out object-type/property-type registry subject matter. Specification §4.15.

label: Mohr 2022 (CODATA 2022)
text: Mohr, P. J.; Newell, D. B.; Taylor, B. N. "CODATA Recommended Values of the Fundamental Physical Constants: 2022." Reviews of Modern Physics 97, 025002 (2025). — Prior-art metrology methodology for convergence computation. Claim 1's convergence analyser is distinguished by the real-time classification into four values and the per-measurer correlation analyser that CODATA does not perform. Specification §2.1.

label: Cecelski 2022 (Cecelski et al. metrology)
text: Cecelski, C. et al. "Metrological reference-value procedures." Metrologia 59, 045002 (2022). — Related metrology methodology. Same mitigation as CODATA 2022.

label: Kusne 2020 (CAMEO autonomous materials)
text: Kusne, A. G. et al. "On-the-fly closed-loop materials discovery via Bayesian active learning (CAMEO)." Nature Communications 11:5966 (2020). — Prior art for closed-loop materials-discovery autonomy. Distinguished by the absence of a measurer-correlation classifier, an admissibility-filtered vocabulary, and an echo-chamber three-way classification.

label: US 2024/0288467 A1
text: U.S. Patent Application Publication No. 2024/0288467 A1, "Autonomous experimentation framework," UT-Battelle LLC / Oak Ridge National Laboratory, published 5 September 2024. — Closed-loop autonomous-experimentation system. Distinguished by the multi-analyser architecture, echo-chamber classifier, and ontology disclaimer of the present invention.

label: Xu 2024 (Amazon RefChecker and FEVEROUS/FEVER)
text: Xu, Y. et al. "RefChecker: Reference-based Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation and Correction." Amazon, 2024. Thorne, J. et al. "FEVER: a large-scale dataset for Fact Extraction and VERification." NAACL 2018, arXiv:1803.05355. Aly, R. et al. "FEVEROUS: Fact Extraction and VERification Over Unstructured and Structured information." NeurIPS 2021, arXiv:2106.05707. — Three-way true/false/unverifiable fact-verification outputs. Claim 2 echo-chamber classifier is distinguished by (i) the requirement of distinct method identifiers; (ii) the trust-weight feedback into measurer classification; (iii) the integration with the coupling map's six-field schema. Specification §4.7.

label: US 12,299,022 B2
text: U.S. Patent No. 12,299,022 B2 (also published as U.S. Patent Application Publication No. 2024/0354322 A1), "Ontology extension via LLM-driven pipeline," Palantir Technologies Inc., priority 24 April 2023, granted 2025. — Newer Palantir family member not carried forward from v6.1. Operates on data through an ontology layer that assigns semantic types (object types, property types) and uses LLM extraction to populate it. Claim 1 and claim 13 tightened disclaimer item (ii) — "no separate object-type or property-type registry external to the storage" — forecloses this reading. Specification §4.13.

label: US 11,714,792 B2
text: U.S. Patent No. 11,714,792 B2, Palantir Technologies Inc., continuation of U.S. Patent No. 7,962,495, granted 1 August 2023. — Newer Palantir continuation not carried forward from v6.1. Same mitigation as US 7,962,495 (v6.1.4).

label: W3C 2017 (W3C SOSA/SSN Recommendation)
text: W3C Semantic Sensor Network Ontology (SOSA/SSN), W3C Recommendation, 19 October 2017. Revised edition 19 July 2023. — Closest standards-side precedent for the pattern "instrument node with characterising property edges alongside observation triples." `sosa:Sensor` is a first-class node; `ssn-system:SystemProperty` edges attach accuracy, sensitivity, measurement range. Distinguished on three points in specification §4.13: (i) SOSA/SSN characterises physical-sensor capability ranges, the invention characterises training-time design choices of computational measurers; (ii) SOSA/SSN uses a specialised `SystemProperty` sub-schema, the invention uses the same six-field directed-entry schema; (iii) SOSA/SSN provides no predictor of divergence between two sensors.

label: Haller 2018 (Haller et al. SOSA/SSN)
text: Haller, A. et al. "The modular SSN ontology: A joint W3C and OGC standard specifying the semantics of sensors, observations, sampling, and actuation." Semantic Web Journal 10(1):9–32, 2018. — Peer-reviewed companion to the W3C SOSA/SSN Recommendation. Same mitigation as v6.2.3.

label: Vrandečić 2014 (Wikidata)
text: Vrandečić, D.; Krötzsch, M. "Wikidata: a free collaborative knowledgebase." Communications of the ACM 57(10):78–85, 2014. — General knowledge-graph representation permitting instruments as first-class items with attribute-type property-value statements. Distinguished in specification §4.13 by the absence of a downstream sensitivity-set-gated divergence-prediction mechanism. Wikidata is a disclosure format; claim 8a is a prediction architecture.

label: Baylor 2017 (TFX ML Metadata)
text: Baylor, D. et al. "TFX: A TensorFlow-based production-scale machine learning platform." Proceedings of the 23rd ACM SIGKDD International Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining, 2017. — ML artefact-and-lineage metadata store with typed `Artifact` / `Execution` / `Context` nodes. Claim 1 tightened disclaimer item (iii) forecloses a separate model-card or model-registry record external to the storage. Specification §4.13.

label: Mitchell 2019 (Model Cards)
text: Mitchell, M. et al. "Model Cards for Model Reporting." Proceedings of the Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAT\* 2019). — Document-level metadata format for ML models. Distinguished in specification §4.13 as a representational register different from the present invention's directed-entry-in-same-storage pattern.

label: Gebru 2021 (Datasheets for Datasets)
text: Gebru, T. et al. "Datasheets for Datasets." Communications of the ACM 64(12):86–92, 2021. — Document-level metadata for datasets. Same mitigation as Model Cards (v6.2.7).

label: JCGM 2008 (JCGM 100:2008 GUM)
text: JCGM 100:2008, Evaluation of measurement data — Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement (GUM). Joint Committee for Guides in Metrology. — Metrological sensitivity coefficients `c_i = ∂f/∂x_i` as structured numerical inputs to uncertainty-propagation code. Claim 8a's sensitivity sets are hand-authored qualitative property-to-attribute dictionaries, not computed Jacobian rows; GUM's construction and claim 8a's construction are orthogonal (uncertainty propagation vs divergence-location prediction). Specification §4.13.

label: Karelina 2023 (Karelina et al. AF)
text: Karelina, M.; Noh, J. J.; Dror, R. O. "How accurately can one predict drug binding modes using AlphaFold models?" eLife 12:RP89386, 2023. — Observational evaluation of AlphaFold on drug-binding-site prediction. Claim 8a is distinguished in specification §4.14 as a pre-registered architectural predictor from attribute-set arithmetic, not a post-hoc observational evaluation.

label: Scardino 2023 (Scardino et al. AF)
text: Scardino, V.; Di Filippo, J. I.; Cavasotto, C. N. "How good are AlphaFold models for docking-based virtual screening?" iScience 26:105832, 2023. — Same mitigation as Karelina (v6.2.10).

label: Terwilliger 2024 (Terwilliger et al. AF)
text: Terwilliger, T. C. et al. "AlphaFold predictions are valuable hypotheses and accelerate but do not replace experimental structure determination." Nature Methods 21:110–116, 2024. — Same mitigation as Karelina (v6.2.10).

label: Schapira 2024 (Schapira et al. AF)
text: Schapira, M. et al. "Big data and benchmarking initiatives to bridge the gap from AlphaFold to drug design." Nature Chemical Biology 20:937–940, 2024. — Same mitigation as Karelina (v6.2.10).

label: Gunasekaran 2024 (Gunasekaran et al. AF)
text: Gunasekaran, R. et al. "The power and pitfalls of AlphaFold2 for structure prediction beyond rigid globular proteins." Nature Chemical Biology 20:950–959, 2024. — Same mitigation as Karelina (v6.2.10).

label: Savojardo 2025 (Savojardo et al. AF/ESMFold)
text: Savojardo, C. et al. "AlphaFold2 and ESMFold: A large-scale pairwise model comparison of human enzymes upon Pfam functional annotation." Journal of Molecular Biology, 2025. — Direct observational comparison of the two predictors that claim 8a treats as the worked example. Distinguished in specification §4.14: observational, post-hoc; not a pre-registered architectural predictor.

label: Fadini 2026 (Fadini et al. AF/ESMFold)
text: Fadini, A. et al. "Comparative evaluation of the prediction accuracy of AlphaFold and ESMFold for monomeric and dimeric proteins." NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics 8(1):lqag002, 2026. — Most-recent AF-vs-ESMFold comparison on 1,666 monomers + 994 dimers. Same mitigation as Savojardo (v6.2.15).

label: Hýsková 2026 (Hýsková et al. structure-prediction)
text: Hýsková, K.; Maršálková, M.; Šimeček, M. "Balancing speed and precision in protein folding: a comparison of AlphaFold2, ESMFold, and OmegaFold." Frontiers in Genetics 16:1715037, 14 January 2026. — Nearest approach to a structure-prediction failure predictor in the observational literature. Distinguished in specification §4.14: predictor inputs are protein-language-model embeddings and pLDDT confidence scores, not architectural-attribute set differences of the comparator instruments themselves.

label: Jiang 2025 (Jiang et al. "Artificial)
text: Jiang, L. et al. "Artificial Hivemind: The Open-Ended Homogeneity of Language Models (and Beyond)." arXiv:2510.22954. NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper Award (Datasets & Benchmarks track). — Highest-profile 2025 work on inter-model output homogeneity across 70+ LLMs. Disclosure is mandatory because post-September-2025 omission of this reference would be conspicuous. Distinguished in specification §4.14: observational homogeneity measurement; claim 8a is a per-property divergence-location predictor.

label: Kuncheva 2003 (Kuncheva & Whitaker ensemble)
text: Kuncheva, L. I.; Whitaker, C. J. "Measures of diversity in classifier ensembles and their relationship with the ensemble accuracy." Machine Learning 51(2):181–207, 2003. — Canonical reference for ensemble-diversity measures (Q-statistic, disagreement measure, double-fault, correlation, kappa, six non-pairwise measures). Distinguished in specification §4.14: all such measures operate on agreement patterns over model *outputs on a labelled dataset*; claim 8a operates on symmetric difference of *architectural attribute sets of the models as entities*. Orthogonal input domains.

label: Kim 2025 (Kim, Garg, Peng, Garg)
text: Kim, J.; Garg, A.; Peng, H.; Garg, V. "Quantifying Correlations of Machine Learning Models." arXiv:2506.07962, June 2025. ICML 2025. — LLM correlated-error mathematical framework. Distinguished in specification §4.14: claim 8a is a system, not a mathematical framework.

label: Goel 2025 (Goel et al. CAPA)
text: Goel, S. et al. "CAPA: Conditional Agreement Plots for Aligning ML Models." arXiv:2502.04313, February 2025. — Model-agreement visualisation. Distinguished as above.

label: Liu 2025 (Liu et al. GATE)
text: Liu, Y. et al. "GATE: Graph-Aware Tensor Extraction for biomedical NLP." Bioinformatics Advances 5(1):vbaf180, 2025. — Biomedical extraction framework. Distant from claim 8a.

label: Fadini 2025 (Fadini et al. Proteins)
text: Fadini, A. et al. Proteins: Structure, Function, and Bioinformatics, 2025. — AF-related structural biology. Related to v6.2.16 (NAR GAB 2026) by author group.

label: Rukhin 2019 (Rukhin metrology)
text: Rukhin, A. L. "Metrological reference-value procedures." Metrologia 56, 035002 (2019). — Metrology methodology. Distinguished same as CODATA 2022 (v6.1.7).

label: Christen 2026 (Christen & Christen record)
text: Christen, P.; Christen, V. "Privacy-preserving record linkage." EDBT 2026, pp. 386–398. — Record-linkage prior art. Distant from claim 1's convergence/correlation analysers.

label: Esward 2025 (Esward et al. metrology)
text: Esward, T. J. et al. Metrology 5(1):3, 2025. — Metrological uncertainty. Distinguished same as CODATA.

label: Kanarik 2023 (Kanarik et al. autonomous)
text: Kanarik, K. J. et al. Nature 616:707, 2023. — Closed-loop autonomy in semiconductor etching. Distant but disclosed for candour.

label: Gibbons 2015 (Gibbons et al. mzRefinery)
text: Gibbons, B. C. et al. "mzRefinery." Bioinformatics 31(23):3838, 2015. — Mass-spectrometry calibration tool. Distant; disclosed for candour.

label: CN117744455A
text: CN117744455A; CN112818137B; CN120145704B. — Non-English-language prior art located during initial search. Full translations not pursued; disclosed for candour.

label: Laumann 2025 (Laumann et al. correlated)
text: Laumann, F. et al. arXiv:2502.03937, February 2025. — Originally cited as correlated-errors prior art. Retained as prudent pending ID verification. If the arXiv ID resolves to the wrong paper as reported by the prior-art audit, the entry is to be corrected or removed before filing.

label: Bhowmick 2024 (Bhowmick et al. 2024)
text: Bhowmick, A. et al. Nature Chemical Biology 20, 2024. — Structural biology evaluation. To be verified as distinct from Schapira 2024 (v6.2.13) and Gunasekaran 2024 (v6.2.14); disclosure is conditional on identifying a separate publication.

label: Wood 2023 (Wood et al. Bregman)
text: Wood, D. et al. "A Unified Theory of Diversity in Ensemble Learning." Journal of Machine Learning Research 24:1–49, 2023. — Extension of Kuncheva & Whitaker ensemble-diversity framework to Bregman and 0/1 losses. Distinguished same as Kuncheva (v6.2.19).

label: Hofman 2023 (Hofman et al. pre-registration)
text: Hofman, J. M.; Chatzimparmpas, A.; Sharma, A.; Watts, D. J.; Hullman, J. "Pre-registration for Predictive Modeling." arXiv:2311.18807, 2023. — Methodological proposal for pre-registration in predictive modelling. Specification §4.14 references the proposal and distinguishes claim 8a as combining pre-registration with architectural-attribute-driven divergence prediction, a combination without located antecedent.

label: Gallitto 2025 (Gallitto et al. external)
text: Gallitto, G. et al. "External validation of machine learning models — registered models and adaptive sample splitting." GigaScience, giaf036, 2025. — Extension of pre-registration to external ML-model validation. Distinguished same as Hofman (v6.2.p2).

label: Lundberg 2017 (SHAP)
text: Lundberg, S. M.; Lee, S.-I. "A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 30, NeurIPS 2017. arXiv:1705.07874. — Per-instance attribution method. Distinguished in specification §4.13 from claim 8a's sensitivity sets: SHAP produces per-instance attributions over outputs; the sensitivity sets are hand-authored property-to-attribute dictionaries consumed upstream by the comparator.

label: Kokhlikyan 2009 (Captum)
text: Kokhlikyan, N. et al. "Captum: A unified and generic model interpretability library for PyTorch." arXiv:2009.07896, 2020. — Same mitigation as SHAP (v6.2.p4).

label: Hall 2022 (GTC metrology)
text: Hall, B. D. "The GUM Tree Calculator: A Python package for measurement modelling and data processing with automatic evaluation of uncertainty." Metrology 2(1):128–149, 2022. — Python implementation of GUM sensitivity-coefficient computation. Distinguished same as JCGM 100:2008 GUM (v6.2.9).

label: Eerlings 2026 (DIVERSE)
text: Eerlings, J. et al. "DIVERSE: Disagreement-Inducing Vector Evolution for Rashomon Set Exploration." ICLR 2026. (Verify canonical venue/URL before filing.) — Recent work on inducing model disagreement for Rashomon-set exploration. Distant from claim 8a but disclosed for candour.
